text: The surgeon performs blunt dissection using robotic instruments, mimicking hand movements typical of open surgery, to navigate the inflamed tissue associated with perforated diverticulitis.
The surgeon uses a black load stapler to transect the rectum, opting not to dissect further due to significant inflammation visible in the surgical field.
The surgeon uses robotic instruments to enter the second abscess identified on the CT scan, visibly draining its contents.
The surgeon tags the rectal stump using sutures, preparing it for identification in a future takedown operation. The vessel sealer is utilized for suturing to minimize instrument costs.
The surgeon brings out the rectal stump and transects it at the skin level, preparing to create an ostomy after removing the diseased portion of the colon.
